entries.title: Classroom grading and student-app polish

items.text: Open a student's attached SpartBoard quiz from Google Classroom and grade it — written responses included — right inside Classroom, then send the grades to your gradebook.
A new Block Copy & Paste setting stops students from copying, cutting, or pasting in the quiz prompt and answer fields.
Students who finish a self-paced quiz attached to Classroom now see their results as soon as you publish, instead of waiting for you to end the quiz.
The SpartBoard screens that appear inside Classroom — for both you and your students — have been redesigned to be clean and on-brand.
Self-paced quizzes and video activities now use a clean, bright light theme; your live, teacher-paced quizzes keep their bold dark look.
Grades you send from a Classroom quiz now reach your gradebook reliably.
A student who already finished a Classroom quiz is taken to their results instead of a "talk to your teacher" dead end.

details.text: Grade quizzes right inside Google Classroom. When you open a student's attached SpartBoard quiz from a Classroom assignment, you now get a grading view inside Classroom — see every student, grade their written responses, publish scores, and send grades to your Classroom gradebook, all without leaving Classroom to go back to your board. Grades arrive in Classroom as drafts that you finish returning with Classroom's own grading tool. (Video activities still open in SpartBoard for review.)

Block Copy & Paste for quizzes. A new test-integrity setting in your quiz settings — just under Tab Switch Detection — stops students from copying, cutting, or pasting in the quiz prompt and answer fields. Turn it on to keep a student from switching to another tab, composing or AI-generating an answer, and pasting a block of text back in. It's off by default, so your existing quizzes behave exactly as before.

Students see their Classroom quiz results without waiting. A student who finishes a self-paced quiz attached to Google Classroom now sees their published results right away, as soon as you've published scores, instead of being stranded on a "waiting for your teacher to end the quiz" screen. Their button reads "View results" once grades are published.

A cleaner, lighter look for self-paced student work. The screens students see when they take a self-paced quiz or video activity on their own time — including through Google Classroom — now use a clean, bright light theme that matches the rest of the student and sign-in experience. Your live, teacher-paced quizzes keep their bold, immersive dark game-show look.

A polished Google Classroom experience. The SpartBoard screens that appear inside Google Classroom — both the setup screen you see when attaching an activity and the screen your students open — have been redesigned to be clean, readable, and on-brand, with all developer and debug information removed.
Grades sent from a Classroom quiz now reach your gradebook reliably. Previously a grade could silently fail to post back to Google Classroom; sending grades now works as expected.

Students who already finished a Classroom quiz no longer hit a dead end. A student who had completed an active quiz attached to Classroom used to see a "you've already taken this quiz, talk to your teacher" message; they're now taken straight to their published results. A student who has used up their attempts sees a clear locked screen inside Classroom rather than being bounced out, and the screenshot watermark on a results screen now identifies the student by name.

entries.title: Delete key, scoring, and translation fixes

details.text: Board canvas — pressing the Delete key while typing in a text field on the board now deletes the character in front of your cursor, the way it does everywhere else. Previously the board treated Delete as a command for the selected widget and swallowed the keystroke, so forward-delete appeared to do nothing while you were editing a widget's settings, a title, or any other text field on the canvas.

Video Activity — a published activity's scores now read correctly when the activity accidentally contains the same question twice (which can happen through a Drive sync hiccup). The duplicate no longer inflates the total possible points, so the percentage students see on a finished, published activity is accurate.

Translations — the admin Sticker Library now appears in German, Spanish, and French. Previously its title, buttons, and descriptions showed raw text keys for admins using one of those languages instead of readable labels.

entries.title: Settings panel, widget, and translation fixes

items.text: Two fixes to the settings panel you reach with a widget's gear icon:
Pressing Escape while typing in a field inside the panel now exits the field instead of closing the whole panel.
A click just outside the panel now reliably closes it, even while the board is busy.
Countdown widgets left running overnight now roll over to the new day at midnight on their own.
Turning sound off mid-spin on the Random widget now takes effect right away, and switching class mid-spin no longer picks a name from the old roster.
Clocks, timers, and other fixed-shape widgets no longer appear stretched when a saved board opens on a differently-shaped screen.
Shared PLC results now score correctly even when a quiz accidentally contains the same question twice.
More of the app — the board navigation menu, class and roster restriction settings, and the PLC workspace — now appears in German, Spanish, and French.

details.text: Widget settings panels (the back of a widget you flip to with the gear icon) now behave the way you'd expect when you press Escape or click away. Pressing Escape while your cursor is in a text field, dropdown, or number box inside the panel now just leaves that field instead of closing the entire panel, so you no longer lose your place mid-edit. And a click just outside the panel now reliably closes it, even while the board is syncing in the background or you've just been dragging widgets around — previously the click was occasionally ignored and the panel stayed open.

Countdown widget — a countdown left open overnight now updates its day count on its own at midnight. Previously a widget that stayed on screen across midnight kept showing the previous day's number until you edited one of its settings or refreshed the page.

Random widget — changes you make while names are still cycling now take effect immediately. Turning sound off mid-spin now silences it right away instead of finishing the spin with sound, and switching to a different class partway through a spin no longer lands on a name from the previous roster.

Widget layout — clocks, timers, and other widgets that keep a fixed shape no longer appear stretched or squished when you open a saved board on a screen shaped differently from the one it was built on. A small number of boards with corrupted sizing information are now repaired automatically so each widget lands at the right size and position.

PLC shared results — student scores now calculate correctly when a quiz accidentally contains the same question twice (which can happen through a Drive sync hiccup). The duplicate no longer inflates the total points or double-counts a correct answer, so the percentages shown in your PLC's shared results are accurate.

Translations — more of the app now appears in German, Spanish, and French. Newly translated areas include the board navigation menu, the live session info, the sidebar's class and roster restriction settings, and the PLC workspace. Previously these showed in English even when you'd chosen another language.

entries.title: Music and SMART Notebook fixes

details.text: Music widget — playing music from your connected Spotify account now starts reliably. Previously, tapping a song right after the widget connected could do nothing at all, leaving you to tap again with no idea why the first try was ignored. Taps you make while the widget is still connecting are now remembered and play automatically the moment it's ready, and repeat and shuffle work as expected once playback begins. (Requires a Spotify Premium account.)

SMART Notebook — stickers and images you place on a page now stay with that page when you reorder or delete pages. Previously, moving pages around left placed items stranded on the wrong page, and deleting a page could leave items pointing at the wrong page or silently vanish. Deleting a page that has placed items now also tells you how many were removed, so nothing disappears without notice.

entries.title: Google Classroom integration

items.text: Attach a SpartBoard quiz or video activity to a Google Classroom assignment, and your students open and take it right inside Classroom.
Connect a SpartBoard class to one of your active Google Classroom courses with the new "Link to Google Classroom" button in your Classes panel.
Two things now flow back to you automatically, with no student personal information ever stored:
Your live monitor shows each student's real name as they join.
Push earned points straight to the Classroom gradebook as draft grades you review and return.

details.text: Google Classroom — you can now attach a SpartBoard quiz or video activity to a Google Classroom assignment from inside Classroom. Students open the assignment and take the quiz or complete the activity right in the Classroom view, without leaving for a separate link or code.

Link to Google Classroom — a new button in your Classes panel lists your active Google Classroom courses and lets you connect any of them to a SpartBoard class in one step. Once linked, that course can attach SpartBoard activities and receive grades.

Named monitor without storing personal info — when students join a Classroom-attached activity, your live monitor shows their real names so you know who's who at a glance. Names are matched from your roster on the fly and are never saved, keeping student personal information private.

Push grades to the Classroom gradebook — the quiz Results monitor gains a "Push grades" button that sends each student's earned points back to the linked Classroom assignment. Scores post as the raw points earned (a 17 out of 20 quiz shows as 17/20, not a percentage) and arrive as draft grades, so you still review and return them in Classroom.

entries.title: Translations, typography, and dice fixes

details.text: Translations — the Dock section headers ("Live", "More", "More widgets", "Open tools", "Create folder") and every label in the What's New modal now appear in German, Spanish, and French. Previously these strings were missing from the non-English translations and either showed as raw text keys or silently fell back to English.

Typography settings — clicking "Inherit" on a widget's font picker now properly clears the per-widget font override so the widget picks up the dashboard's global font. Previously the click saved a placeholder value that could leave the widget stuck on the wrong font and prevent later changes to the global font from taking effect.

Dice widget — changing the number of dice from the settings panel while the dice are still rolling now lands on the correct count. Previously, if you bumped the count from 2 to 4 mid-roll, the animation would finish but only the original 2 dice would be saved.

entries.title: Live quiz answer integrity safeguards

items.text: Student-typed essay and written answers no longer come back blank when you unlock a question for editing or when students back-navigate to a previously-submitted question.
When a question's timer auto-submits a multiple choice answer, the committed option now stays highlighted on the locked screen instead of appearing blank.
Revealing answers immediately after a timer auto-submit no longer shows students a brief flash of "incorrect" feedback before the correct response catches up.

If a student tries to clear a previously-saved answer by deleting all their text, a banner now tells them to use Submit to confirm the clear — previously the deletion was silently blocked and the student had no idea why it didn't stick.

details.text: Live quiz — student-typed essay and written answers no longer disappear when you unlock a question for editing, or when students back-navigate to a previously-submitted question. The student app now keeps every answer in local memory and prefers that over slower cloud syncs, so a late-arriving sync can't wipe out text the student already typed. Reported on a live "Independent Reading Test" essay quiz where students saw their work come back blank after a teacher unlock.

Live quiz — when a question's timer expires and auto-submits a multiple choice answer, the chosen option is now highlighted on the locked screen. Previously the answer was committed correctly but the highlight was missing, making the locked screen look as if nothing had been chosen.

Live quiz — when you reveal answers right after a timer auto-submit, students no longer see a brief flash of "incorrect" feedback before the correct response catches up. The reveal now reads from the same local cache the student just committed against, so the feedback matches what they actually answered.

Live quiz — if a student tries to clear a previously-saved answer by deleting all the text in the field, the app now surfaces a banner asking them to use Submit to confirm the clear. Previously, the deletion was silently refused as a safeguard against accidental loss, and the student had no UI signal explaining why their clear didn't stick.

entries.title: Shared notebook hotspots and Boards modal copy

details.text: Shared notebooks — page-link hotspots no longer disappear when a colleague imports a shared notebook. If you authored a notebook with objects that jump to another page on Ctrl/Cmd+click, those hotspots now travel with the share, and the teacher who imports the copy gets the same jumps wired up to the same objects.

Boards modal — the header now reads "Boards & Collections" instead of just "Boards", matching the search placeholder copy already in place and making it clearer that collections live alongside individual boards in the same modal.

entries.title: Quiz safeguards, Scoreboard layout, and Random widget chip

items.text: Pausing a live quiz now also pauses the 90-minute idle auto-submit, so you can hold a session over lunch, a fire drill, or until tomorrow's class without students being force-submitted while you're away.

Students sitting in the lobby of a quiz you haven't launched yet no longer get force-submitted with empty answers if you get pulled away before starting question 1. Lobby sessions left untouched for more than 24 hours are treated as abandoned and finalized normally.
Resuming a paused session gives every still-joined student a fresh 90-minute idle window, so a long pause never causes their work to auto-submit the moment they reconnect.

The Cards vs Rows layout toggle moved out of the Scoreboard's chrome and into the Settings panel, where it sits alongside team and color settings — easier to find when you're setting up a board for the first time.

The active class chip and the Mark Absent button merged into a single icon-only chip with a popover. Click the target icon to see the class name, switch class, or mark absent students — frees up header room at narrow widget widths.

details.text: Live quiz — paused sessions are now exempt from the 90-minute idle auto-submit. If you pause a quiz at the end of a class period to resume the next day (or step out for lunch, a fire drill, or a hallway conversation), students' in-progress attempts are preserved instead of being force-submitted while the session is paused.

Live quiz — lobby attendees are no longer force-submitted while the session sits in the waiting state. If you create a quiz, students join the lobby, and you get pulled away before launching question 1, those students stay in the lobby with their join intact instead of being auto-finalized with 0 answers after 90 minutes. Lobby sessions that sit untouched for more than 24 hours are treated as abandoned and swept on the next cron pass, so old practice/demo sessions don't accumulate as never-finalized rows.

Live quiz — when you resume a paused session, the 90-minute idle clock restarts for every still-joined and in-progress student before the session flips back to active. Even if the pause lasted overnight, students get a full fresh window to keep working when they reconnect, instead of being immediately auto-submitted because their last activity was hours or days ago.

Scoreboard widget — the Cards / Rows layout toggle moved from the widget header into the Settings panel as a labelled control. Existing scoreboards keep whichever layout they were already using; the header is now reserved for the score display itself.

Random widget — the active class chip and the Mark Absent button merged into a single icon-only chip with a popover. The popover shows the class name, lets you switch class, and offers a Mark Absent Students action when the Randomizer is pointing at a real class roster (the action hides itself when you're in custom-names mode). Stations, Seating Chart, and Lunch Count keep their separate chips for now.

entries.title: Quiz reliability, grading fixes, and translations

items.text: Student answers to every question type — multiple choice, fill in the blank, matching, and ordering — now save automatically as students work, so closing a tab mid-attempt no longer drops their progress.
Popping a quiz into a new browser tab no longer locks students out with a "PIN already joined" error — the new tab now retries and recovers automatically.
Abandoned quiz attempts auto-submit after 90 minutes of inactivity instead of sitting in limbo on the teacher's monitor.
Removing a student from a live quiz now archives their partial work so you can recover their answers later, instead of deleting them.
"Send Groups → Stations" from the Random widget now uses the group names you set (or "Group 1", "Group 2", …) instead of long, cryptic ID strings.
Drawing strokes no longer cut off when your pointer briefly crosses the edge of the canvas mid-stroke.
Video Activity scoring no longer counts duplicate questions twice against a student's possible score — a fully correct answer now reads 100% even when the question list contains repeats.
PLC panels (titles, buttons, dialogs, and the Remote Control link) now show in German, Spanish, and French instead of silently falling back to English.
Set per-building Number Line defaults for font family, text color, surface color, and surface opacity from Admin Settings — new Number Line widgets in that building start with your chosen look.

details.text: Live quiz — student answers to multiple choice, fill in the blank, matching, and ordering questions now autosave as the student works, the same way written responses already did. If a student closes their tab, switches devices, or loses connection mid-attempt, the answers they had entered are preserved and reappear when they rejoin.

Live quiz — popping a quiz out into its own browser window or tab no longer triggers a "PIN already joined" lockout for the student. The new tab now retries through a brief network blip and the lockout message points students at closing the other tab first instead of asking the teacher for help.

Live quiz — abandoned attempts that sit untouched for 90 minutes are now auto-submitted in the background and marked complete, instead of staying as "in progress" on the teacher's monitor forever. Whatever the student had answered is kept; unanswered questions stay blank.
Live quiz — when you remove a student from a session, their partial work is now archived rather than deleted, so you can recover their answers if they were removed by mistake.
Random widget — "Send Groups → Stations" now creates stations labeled with the group names you saved (or "Group 1", "Group 2", …) instead of long ID strings.
Annotation canvas — fast strokes whose motion briefly crosses the canvas edge no longer commit prematurely. The stroke now stays in progress until you actually lift your pointer.

Video Activity grading — if the same question appears twice in an activity (for example, through a Drive sync hiccup), the duplicate no longer inflates the maximum possible score. A fully correct answer now reads 100% in those cases instead of 50%.
Translations — the PLC (Professional Learning Community) sidebar panel and Remote Control sidebar link now display in German, Spanish, and French, instead of silently falling back to English.

Admin Settings — the Number Line widget configuration panel gains an "Appearance Defaults" section, so admins can set per-building default font family, text color, surface color, and surface opacity. Teachers in that building get those defaults the next time they add a new Number Line widget.

entries.title: Notebook editor: shapes, links, copy/paste, and undo

items.text: Add text boxes and shapes — rectangle, ellipse, line, and arrow — straight from the editor toolbar.
Link any object on a page to another page in the same notebook, then Ctrl/Cmd+click it during a lesson to jump there.
Copy and paste objects across notebook pages — any page-jump links travel with the paste.
Paste images and text straight from your operating system clipboard onto the current page.
Undo and redo any edit with Cmd/Ctrl+Z and Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+Z — each page keeps its own 50-step history.
Set a per-page background:
Solid color from an 8-swatch palette or a custom color.
Pattern overlay — lines, grid, or dots.
Your own uploaded image.
Reorder layers with a new toolbar cluster or with Cmd/Ctrl+] and Cmd/Ctrl+[ shortcuts.
Rotate any selected object with a new top handle.
The eraser now removes any object under your cursor — not just ink.
Toolbar collapsed into six popover-driven tools with continuous size sliders, and white was added to the color palette.
Notebook autosave runs quietly behind the scenes without ever clobbering a live drag, resize, or text edit.
Specialist Schedule widget gains a proper Appearance tab for typography, colors, and text size.
Random widget result text now sizes to the winner shown — short results like "1." fill the widget.
Random and Stations widget buttons no longer collapse on small widget sizes.
YouTube playlist URLs embed correctly even when the playlist ID comes before the video ID.
Guided Learning matching questions no longer mark a student correct when they include extra wrong pairs.
AI quiz and mini-app generation no longer fails to parse when the AI returns a list of items.
"Saved", "Success", and "Error" messages now show in German, Spanish, and French.

details.text: Notebook editor — new text and shape tools. The toolbar now has a Text tool that drops an editable text box anywhere on the page, plus a Shapes button hiding rectangle, ellipse, line, and arrow tools. Each shape and text object joins the same select / move / resize / rotate / delete / link pipeline that drawn objects already use, so you can manipulate the whole page the same way.

Notebook editor — object-to-page hyperlinks. Link any object on a notebook page to another page in the same notebook. A jump-to-link button (arrow) appears next to the manage-link button on linked objects, Ctrl/Cmd+clicking a linked object follows the link, and the link picker has a "Go to page N" button so you can verify the target without leaving editing mode. The picker also no longer dismisses itself the moment you open it.
Notebook editor — page-jump hyperlinks preserved on SMART Notebook import. Imported decks now carry over their original page-to-page links, so existing lessons keep working without re-wiring.

Notebook editor — cross-page copy and paste with link preservation. Cmd/Ctrl+C copies any selection, Cmd/Ctrl+V drops it onto the page you're currently viewing (even a different page), and any object-to-page links travel with the paste — clones automatically point at the right page. Cmd/Ctrl+D in-page duplicates also carry links forward.

Notebook editor — paste from your operating system clipboard. Cmd/Ctrl+V with an image on your clipboard drops the image onto the current page as a movable object. Pasted text becomes an editable text object at page center. When both clipboards have content, the in-app clipboard wins so a stale screenshot can't replace a notebook copy.

Notebook editor — undo and redo. Every edit — drawing, shape, text, move, resize, rotate, delete, paste, layer change — is now an undoable step. Cmd/Ctrl+Z walks back, Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+Z (or Ctrl+Y) walks forward, and each page keeps its own 50-step history that starts from the page as you loaded it.

Notebook editor — per-page background picker. Pick a solid color from an 8-swatch palette (or a custom color), overlay a pattern (lines, grid, or dots), or upload your own background image. Backgrounds save with the page so the notebook stays self-contained when you share it.

Notebook editor — layer ordering. Reorder any selected object with a new four-button Layers cluster in the toolbar — bring to front, bring forward, send backward, send to back — or with Cmd/Ctrl+], Cmd/Ctrl+[, Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+], and Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+[.
Notebook editor — rotation handle on selected objects. A new handle at the top of the selection box rotates the object, alongside the existing eight resize handles.

Notebook editor toolbar redesigned. Nine inline tools, five colors, and three width buttons collapsed into six top-level tools, each with its own popover. Shapes live behind a Shapes button. Size is now a continuous slider — 4 to 80 pixels for pen, highlighter, shapes, and eraser, and 12 to 96 pt for text. White was added to the palette. Popovers are opaque so they don't blend into the page underneath.

Notebook eraser now removes any object under the cursor. Previously the eraser only cleared ink — it now also removes shapes, text boxes, and images, with the new eraser-size slider driving a multi-sample hit radius.

Notebook autosave never clobbers a live edit. The editor is now strictly write-only after mount: autosave can run as often as it wants without ever overwriting an in-flight drag, resize, or text edit. This eliminates a class of "my work got reverted" and "the page snapped back" bugs.

Notebook Text tool — focus and second-drop fixes. Clicking outside an active text object no longer drops a second empty text on the next click. Initial typing into a new text object lands reliably. Clicking an existing text object while the Text tool is active opens it for editing instead of creating a new one over the top.

Specialist Schedule widget now has a proper Appearance tab. Typography, font color, card color, and text size presets all live in a dedicated Appearance settings panel. A redundant General tab was removed so the settings match the rest of the widgets in the dock.

Random widget result text now sizes to what's actually displayed. Short results like "1." fill the widget instead of being pinned to the size of the longest roster name, and three sizing regimes (spinning, settled, placeholder) make each state look intentional.

Random and Stations widget controls no longer collapse on small widget sizes. Chips, buttons, and icons recover their minimum size floors that an earlier sizing sweep had erased — Shuffle, Rotate, and Reset on the Stations widget are usable again on small layouts.
YouTube playlist URLs now embed correctly even when the playlist ID appears before the video ID in the URL — copying the URL straight from a playlist page no longer breaks the embed.

Guided Learning matching questions are now graded strictly. A student who submitted every correct pair plus one extra wrong pair was previously getting full credit; the answer is now marked wrong unless the submission matches the expected pairs exactly.

AI-generated content (quiz items, mini-app pieces, and other Gemini-driven outputs) no longer fails to parse when the AI returns a top-level list of items instead of an object. "Couldn't read the AI response" errors on otherwise valid generations should be gone.
"Saved", "Success", and "Error" status messages now display in your selected language. Missing translation keys were added to German, Spanish, and French locales.

Record, Magic, and Remote dock buttons now respect the right permission gate. An admin disabling a per-widget permission for these toolbar actions no longer hides them — they correctly follow their own feature-permission setting.

entries.title: The Draw widget becomes a full whiteboard

items.text: Add shapes — rectangles, ellipses, lines, and arrows — from a new Tools cluster in the Draw toolbar.
Drop in a text box anywhere on the canvas and edit it in place.
Add images by pasting, dragging a file onto the canvas, or clicking the Image button.
Pick the Select tool to move, resize, rotate, or delete any object on the canvas.
Build multi-page Draw widgets and flip between pages from a page strip in the toolbar.
Undo and redo any change with Ctrl/Cmd+Z and Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+Z.
Export the Draw widget as a PNG image or PDF.
Choose a per-page background template: Blank, Grid, Lines, or Dots.
Draw widgets stay smooth with hundreds of shapes, text boxes, and images across many pages.
Widget settings panels stay anchored next to their widget when you zoom the board.
Specialist Schedule items missing a complete time no longer show up as already finished.
The Boards Manager color picker now shows the right labels in German, Spanish, and French.
Instructional Routines step badges follow your board's theme color.
Widgets with partially-migrated layouts now land in the right spot on screen.

details.text: Shape tools on the Draw widget — add rectangles, ellipses, lines, and arrows. The toolbar now has a six-button Tools cluster (Pen, Eraser, Line, Arrow, Rectangle, Ellipse), and a Shape Fill toggle in settings switches new shapes between filled and outline-only. The annotation overlay that lets you draw on top of any board gets the same shape tools.

Text boxes on the Draw widget — pick the new Text tool, click anywhere on the canvas, and start typing. Text commits when you click away or press Cmd-Enter, and you can edit a text box again by double-clicking it. Font, size, and color come from the widget's typography settings.

Images on the Draw widget — paste an image from the clipboard, drag a file onto the canvas, or click the new Image button in the toolbar. All three drop the image where you placed it, sized to fit, and uploads sync to your board.

Select, move, resize, and rotate on the Draw widget — switch to the new Select tool to grab any object on the canvas. Eight handles let you resize from any edge or corner, a top handle rotates, dragging the body moves it, Backspace or Delete removes the selection, and the arrow keys nudge by 1px (10px with Shift).

Multi-page Draw widgets — a single Draw widget can now hold any number of pages. A page strip across the toolbar lets you flip between pages, and a kebab menu on each page chip handles add, delete, and reorder. Each page keeps its own contents and background.

Undo and redo on the Draw widget — every drawing, shape, text edit, image insertion, move, resize, rotate, and delete is now an undoable step. Ctrl/Cmd+Z steps back, Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+Z (or Ctrl+Y) steps forward, and Undo and Redo buttons in the toolbar do the same. Clear All counts as a single step you can fully undo.

Export Draw widgets — save the current page as a PNG, save every page as PNGs one at a time, or open a print dialog with one printable page per Draw page that you can save as a PDF. Selection handles are never baked into the exported image.

Background templates on the Draw widget — each page can use a Blank, Grid, Lines, or Dots background. Pick the template in settings; the active page updates right away and your choice becomes the default for new pages on that widget.

Draw widgets stay fast with lots of content. Each shape, text box, or image now syncs to the cloud on its own instead of rewriting the whole board every time you draw, so Draw widgets with hundreds of objects across many pages stay responsive while you work.

Widget settings panel positioning. Opening a widget's settings panel while the board is zoomed now anchors the panel correctly next to its widget. Previously the panel could land in the wrong spot when the board's zoom didn't match the default.

Specialist Schedule items with missing or malformed time data no longer appear permanently as finished — with a strikethrough, green checkmark, and greyed background — before they actually occur. They now show as upcoming until their real time elapses.

The color picker in the Boards Manager (used when assigning a color to a collection) now shows the correct labels in German, Spanish, and French. Previously these languages were missing the new picker's strings and showed raw key names.
The numbered step badge in the Instructional Routines widget now uses your board's primary theme color instead of ignoring it.

Widgets whose stored position was partially converted to the newer proportional layout now render at the correct spot. Previously a partial conversion could leave a widget at the wrong on-screen position until you moved it by hand.

entries.title: Clock building defaults and widget polish

items.text: Admins can now set a default Clock display style (Modern, LCD, or Minimal) and a Glow Effect for each building, so new Clock widgets land in the look your school prefers.
Timers and stopwatches now run smoothly while you drag or edit other widgets on the board.
The Mini App toolbar scales with the widget — buttons, icons, and labels stay properly sized when you resize a running mini app.
A QR widget syncing its URL from a Text widget no longer writes to the cloud on every unrelated board change.
Turning off "sync URL from a Text widget" on a QR widget now keeps the URL you'd typed in before — your saved address is no longer wiped.

details.text: Clock building defaults now include the default display style (Modern, LCD, or Minimal) and a Glow Effect toggle. Admins can set these per building from the Clock configuration panel, so new Clock widgets in each building start with the look you want — alongside the existing time format, font, and theme color defaults.
Timers and stopwatches run smoother on busy boards. The countdown no longer hitches or skips when you're dragging or editing other widgets while a timer is running.
The Mini App toolbar that appears when an app is running now scales with the widget. Buttons, icons, labels, and spacing stay properly sized as you make the mini app widget larger or smaller.

QR widgets paired with a Text widget (the "sync URL from a Text widget" option) are now much lighter on the cloud — they no longer save behind the scenes every time anything on the board changes. The QR code still updates live as you edit the linked text.

Turning off the "sync URL from a Text widget" option on a QR widget now keeps whatever URL you had typed in before turning sync on. Previously, toggling sync on and then off would replace your saved URL with the synced text.

entries.title: A streamlined PLC quiz and video workspace

items.text: Quizzes and Video Activities each now have their own Library, In-Progress, and Completed tabs, so shared content and the assignments running from it live together — the separate Assignments page is gone.
Shared quizzes and saved assignment setups are now one combined Library, with an "Assign to my classes" action on every quiz.
Assign buttons sit right at the top of each section, so starting a new assignment is one click away.
Monitor a running assignment or check its results — for both quizzes and video activities — without leaving the PLC.

details.text: The PLC Quizzes and Video Activities sections each now have their own Library, In-Progress, and Completed tabs, so a section's shared content and the assignments running from it live in one place. The separate Assignments page is gone — everything you did there now happens right inside the Quizzes and Video Activities sections.

The PLC quiz library is now a single list. Shared quizzes and saved assignment setups are combined into one Library, and every quiz has an "Assign to my classes" action, so you can hand a colleague's shared quiz to your own students in one step — its run settings come along with it.
Assign buttons now sit at the top of the Quizzes and Video Activities sections, so starting a new assignment is one click from wherever you are in the PLC.

Monitor an in-progress assignment or view its results without leaving the PLC. Both quizzes and video activities now open their live monitor and results in a clean window on top of the PLC space, instead of closing the PLC page first.

entries.title: Built-in quiz settings and notebook editing upgrades

items.text: Set how a quiz or video activity behaves — pacing, integrity and feedback options, attempt limits, and gamification — right in the editor, saved with the quiz so you set it up once.
Share a quiz with your PLC and your settings travel with it — members get your questions and your settings, with nothing to re-configure.
In-progress assignments now have Monitor and Results on hand, and teammates can run a colleague's live assignment in their own classes in one step.
Assigning is now just picking your classes and a due date — everything else comes from the quiz, and assignments already running keep the settings they launched with.
Share a notebook with a paste-able link — anyone you send it to gets their own copy of the pages and assets to use and edit.
Drag assets straight onto the page as movable, resizable objects that stay put through resizing, full-screen, and reopening.
Select several objects at once by dragging a box around them, then move, delete, or duplicate them together.
Rename notebooks from the library and switch between card and list views.
Maximized widgets get a cleaner corner toolbar — settings, screenshot, annotate, and record — instead of a bar that popped up on every tap.
Notebook page editing is smoother: thin lines and text boxes are easier to select, edited text no longer overlaps, and the cursor starts at the end of the text.

details.text: Quiz and Video Activity settings now live with the content itself. Open the editor and use the new Settings tab to choose how an assignment behaves — paced mode, the integrity, feedback, and shuffle options, attempt limits, and gamification or scoring — right alongside your questions. The settings save with the quiz, so you set them once instead of re-entering them every time you assign.

When you share a quiz or video activity with your PLC, your behavior settings now travel with it. Members receive your questions and your settings together, so no one has to re-configure how the assignment runs, and editing the shared quiz updates everyone's copy.

In-progress assignments give you more to work with: Monitor and Results are available right where you manage assignments, and a teammate can take a colleague's live assignment to their own classes in one step — since the questions and settings are already shared, there's nothing to re-enter.

Assigning a quiz or video activity is now a quick two-step: pick the class period(s) and set a due date. How the assignment behaves comes from the quiz's own settings, shown as a summary with an "Edit in quiz" link if you want to change it. Assignments that have already launched keep the settings they started with, so editing a quiz only affects future assignments.

Share a SMART Notebook with a paste-able link. A Share button on each notebook — in the library and in the open notebook — copies a link, and anyone you send it to can paste it to get their own copy of the pages and assets to use and edit.

Drag assets straight onto the notebook page. Assets from the panel now drop onto the page as movable, resizable objects that stay with the page, instead of landing as a separate sticker that could hide behind the notebook when full-screen. Their positions hold through resizing, maximizing, and closing and reopening the notebook.

Select several objects on a notebook page at once by dragging a box around them, then move, delete, or duplicate the whole group together. Shift-click adds or removes individual objects from the selection.

Rename imported notebooks from the library — a new edit action on each notebook card lets you give it a clearer title — and switch the library between card and list views, with your choice remembered per notebook.

A cleaner full-screen toolbar. When you maximize a widget, the floating toolbar that used to pop up on every interaction is gone, replaced by a tidy button in the corner that expands to the actions that still matter full-screen: settings, screenshot, annotate, and screen record.
Thin lines and text boxes are now easier to select on a notebook page — a near-miss click snaps to the closest object instead of starting a selection box.

Editing text on a notebook page no longer crams or overlaps longer text, the cursor now starts at the end of the text when you open it for editing, and the selection highlight lines up correctly with the object you picked.

entries.title: SMART Notebook lessons and a PLC redesign

items.text: Import your existing SMART Notebook lessons — pages keep their original order and embedded images render correctly.
Open any page to edit it: move, resize, duplicate, or retype objects, and add, delete, or reorder pages.
Write on slides live with pen, highlighter, and eraser tools.
Too big to import? A built-in converter shrinks large lessons right in your browser — no install needed.
A redesigned PLC space with side navigation and a clean Home dashboard.
Author quizzes and video activities and set up assignments without ever leaving the PLC.
A filterable view of shared student results, embedded Google Docs for shared notes, and one-click resource import into your PLC library.
Admins can push curated resources and assignments to specific PLCs or to all of them at once.
Your Style, Preferences, and Language options are now grouped into a single Settings modal instead of separate sidebar panels.
Music widget playback controls — play, skip, repeat, and shuffle — now look and work the same across every view, and the tab pills fill the bar and shrink to icons on narrow widgets.
PLC quiz and video-activity assignments sync reliably again after silently failing for some teachers.

details.text: SMART Notebook import — bring your existing SMART Notebook lessons into SpartBoard. Pages now arrive in their true display order (instead of being scrambled), and embedded images that previously showed up blank render faithfully.

Edit imported pages — open any page in the editor to select an object and move it, resize it from the corners, duplicate it, or double-click to retype its text. You can also add a blank page, delete a page, or reorder pages within a lesson, and every change is saved to the cloud.

Write on slides — a floating pen, highlighter, and eraser palette lets you annotate any imported page live, the way you would on a SMART board. Your ink becomes a normal object you can move, resize, or delete, and it sticks around when you save.

Built-in lesson converter — if a SMART Notebook file is too large to import directly, a styled prompt now opens a converter that shrinks it entirely in your browser (no download or install required) and hands back an optimized file ready to import. A real 14 MB, 230-page deck compresses to about 6 MB with no visible quality loss.

PLC collaborative space redesign — the PLC dashboard moves from a row of tabs to a clear side-rail navigation with a redesigned Home page. Members and quick-create actions now live in the page header, and the dashboard fills the full width of the screen.
Author and assign inside the PLC — create quizzes and video activities and fully configure their assignments right within the PLC, without first building them on a board.

Shared data, notes, and resources — browse shared student results in a filterable view (by type, assignment, teacher, class, or date), keep shared notes in an embedded Google Doc, and pull a resource into your PLC library or jump to its assignment with a single click.
Admin-curated PLC resources — admins can push selected resources and assignments to specific PLCs or to every PLC at once, making it easy to roll shared materials out across teams.

Unified Settings modal — your Style, Preferences, and Language & Region options now live together in one focused Settings modal with its own Appearance, Dock, Behavior, and Language sections, instead of being split across separate sidebar panels. Dock position and dock styling sit side by side, and clear "This board" / "All boards" labels show whether a setting applies everywhere or just to the current board.

Music widget controls — play, pause, skip, repeat, and shuffle now behave consistently across the compact bar, minimal view, and full player. The tab pills span the full width of the bar and collapse to clean icons on narrow widgets instead of truncating their labels.
Admin Settings now uses the same side-rail navigation for moving between sections.

PLC assignment sync — quizzes and video activities created with PLC sync turned on now record correctly again. A recent change to how those assignments are stored had been silently blocking the sync for affected teachers.
Music widget repeat and shuffle now recover on their own if Spotify briefly loses track of the active device, instead of appearing stuck in the wrong state.

entries.title: Boards card actions and follow-up fixes

items.text: Share and Duplicate icons now sit directly on every Board and Collection card — no more digging into the right-click menu on tablets and projector touchscreens.
Duplicate a whole Collection at once — name, color, and every board inside come along (sub-collections are left in place).
Duplicate feels instant: the new card appears in the grid immediately, the icon shows a spinner while the save finishes, and rapid clicks no longer create extra copies.
The new Backgrounds modal now tracks built-in colors, patterns, and gradients in Recents (not just custom uploads).
If a background upload is rejected, picking the same file again now works instead of appearing stuck.
Record and Magic can be removed from Quick Access slots again after they were chosen.
Quiz and Video Activity now catch students clicking into the browser's URL bar or bookmark dropdown — recent browser changes had quietly let some focus-loss events slip past.
Text Widget bullet and numbered list toolbar buttons now apply formatting on a fresh single-line block (keyboard shortcuts were unaffected).

details.text: Boards manager — Share and Duplicate icons now sit alongside Pin on every Board and Collection card, so the most common actions are one tap away on tablets and projector touchscreens. The Share icon honors your existing dashboard-sharing permission setting, and the right-click menu still works exactly the same way.

Duplicate a Collection — copy a whole Collection (name + color) along with every board inside it in one action. Sub-collections are left in place so the operation stays bounded; you can re-run Duplicate on any sub-folder you also want to copy.

Duplicate (Boards and Collections) now feels instant — the new card appears in the grid immediately, the icon flips to a spinner while the save finishes in the background, and rapid taps no longer queue up multiple copies. If the save fails, the optimistic card is removed and the existing error toast still fires.

Quiz and Video Activity tab-switch detection now catches clicks into the browser's URL bar, bookmark dropdown, devtools, and other browser-chrome targets. Recent Chrome and Firefox changes had quietly stopped firing focus-loss events for those targets, so a few students could leave the tab without triggering the warning overlay. The published-results view is intentionally unchanged so a quick URL-bar click on a posted score doesn't lock students out.

Text Widget bullet-list and numbered-list toolbar buttons now apply the list formatting on a fresh single-line text block instead of just showing the button as active. Keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+Shift+7 / Ctrl+Shift+8) were not affected.
Backgrounds modal — built-in colors, patterns, and gradients are now tracked in Recents alongside custom uploads, instead of only My Uploads showing up there.
Backgrounds upload — if a file is rejected (too large, Drive not ready, etc.), selecting the same file again now triggers a new upload attempt instead of silently doing nothing.
Quick Access Widgets modal — Record and Magic can now be removed from your slots once added. They were previously stranded in the selected list with no way to deselect.

entries.title: Refreshed sidebar modals

items.text: Backgrounds — a new full modal with Favorites, Recents, dynamic Media categories, Colors, Patterns, Gradients, and My Uploads. Click the star on any background to favorite it.
Quick Access Widgets — new modal with a search bar, a 6-column grid, and an "X/2 slots" counter that updates as you pick.
Roster editor now shows PIN, first name, last name, and restrictions by default, and toggle colors match the rest of the app (colored = on, gray = off).
Sidebar Style panel applies every change live — the Save/Discard step is gone and sliders feel smoother.
Modals, popovers, banners, toasts, and menus across the app now fade and slide in as designed instead of popping in instantly.

details.text: Backgrounds picker redesigned — new full modal with a left rail of Favorites, Recents, Media categories, Colors, Patterns, Gradients, and My Uploads. Star any background to favorite it, and recently used backgrounds are tracked automatically. Custom colors, gradients, and Drive uploads all live in the same place.
Quick Access Widgets redesigned — new modal with sticky search, a 6-column grid, an "X/2 slots" counter, and live-apply when you click a tile.

What's New panel redesigned with a curated, themed overview by default and a "Read full update" link for the full patch notes. The date now formats in your language and the redundant version label is gone.
Admins can tag background presets directly from the Background Manager (suggestions are pulled from your existing tags) to keep large libraries organized and easier to find.
Admins can restrict any global feature to specific buildings in Global Settings — roll a feature out school by school instead of district-wide.

Roster (My Classes) editor — PIN, first name, last name, and restrictions are visible by default, and the toggle button colors flipped to match the rest of the app (colored = on, gray = off). Email stays off by default for privacy.
Sidebar Style panel — removed the Save/Discard footer; every control now applies live as you change it, with smoother sliders.
Animations restored — modals, popovers, banners, toasts, FAB menus, and sidebar tabs now fade and slide in as designed instead of mounting instantly.

entries.title: Collections, sharing, and a Boards manager refresh

items.text: Group your boards into folders so a busy dashboard list stays organized.
Pick a color, then drag boards into the Collection from the Boards manager.
Share a whole Collection with a colleague, or save it as a template to reuse next year.
Pin frequently used boards so they appear at the top regardless of which Collection is active.
Two new options when publishing quiz results for student review:
Watermark — a faint identifier behind each student's responses so screenshots are traceable.
Tab-navigation lock — pick how many times a student can leave the results tab before it locks.
Unlock one student at a time without reopening the whole assignment.
Every card shows a visual preview of its widgets, and you can drag from anywhere on the card.
Collections and Boards each get their own button instead of a shared kebab menu.

details.text: Collections — group your boards into folders, share a whole folder with another teacher, or save one as a template to reuse next year.
Pin boards across Collections — your favorites now appear at the top of the bottom-left Boards menu no matter which folder they live in.
Quiz results — watermark + warning if students try to screenshot, and you can now unlock one student at a time without reopening the whole assignment.
Boards manager refreshed — every card now shows a visual preview of the widgets, you can drag from anywhere on the card, and Collections/Boards each get their own button instead of a single kebab.
Quieter Google Drive reconnect prompt — one banner per session instead of one for every failed sync.
Quiz rapid-tap lockout now fires reliably on the first tap — answers no longer slip past after a fast double-tap.
Sound Widget no longer gets stuck silent after switching browser tabs.
Text editor selection now extends across paragraphs and list items instead of stopping at the first block.

entries.title: What's New panel introduced

details.text: Click the new "What's New" link on the Update Available toast — or open it any time from the bottom of the sidebar menu — to see a friendly summary of what changed before you refresh.
